name: Algebraic Geometry
description: Schemes, sheaves, cohomology, moduli spaces, mirror symmetry

results.name: Grothendieck's Theory of Schemes
results.structural_conclusion: Algebraic varieties are fundamentally local-global objects that can be understood through their local rings, unifying algebraic and geometric perspectives. The scheme-theoretic approach reveals that geometric objects naturally carry arithmetic information through their coordinate rings.
results.source_citation: Grothendieck, A. Éléments de géométrie algébrique, Publ. Math. IHÉS

results.name: Grothendieck-Riemann-Roch Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Topological and algebraic invariants of algebraic varieties are fundamentally connected through characteristic classes. This reveals that geometric properties can be computed through purely algebraic cohomological methods.
results.source_citation: Grothendieck, A. Sur quelques points d'algèbre homologique, Tohoku Math. J.

results.name: Cohomology of Coherent Sheaves
results.structural_conclusion: Global geometric properties of algebraic varieties are encoded in the cohomology of their structure sheaves. This establishes that local algebraic data can be assembled to recover global geometric invariants.
results.source_citation: Serre, J.-P. Faisceaux algébriques cohérents, Ann. of Math.

results.name: Resolution of Singularities
results.structural_conclusion: Every algebraic variety can be transformed into a smooth variety through a sequence of blowups, revealing that singularities are not fundamental obstructions but can be systematically resolved. This shows that smooth varieties are the natural building blocks of algebraic geometry.
results.source_citation: Hironaka, H. Resolution of singularities of an algebraic variety, Ann. of Math.

results.name: Riemann-Roch Theorem for Curves
results.structural_conclusion: The dimension of spaces of meromorphic functions on algebraic curves is governed by a universal arithmetic formula involving genus and divisor degree. This reveals that complex analysis and algebraic geometry share fundamental structural principles.
results.source_citation: Riemann, B. Theorie der Abel'schen Funktionen, J. Reine Angew. Math.

results.name: Weil Conjectures
results.structural_conclusion: The arithmetic properties of algebraic varieties over finite fields are controlled by topological invariants, establishing a deep connection between number theory and algebraic topology. This reveals that geometric and arithmetic structures are fundamentally intertwined.
results.source_citation: Deligne, P. La conjecture de Weil I, Publ. Math. IHÉS

results.name: Kodaira Vanishing Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Positivity conditions on line bundles force the vanishing of higher cohomology groups, establishing that geometric positivity translates directly into algebraic simplification. This reveals a fundamental duality between geometric and cohomological complexity.
results.source_citation: Kodaira, K. On a differential-geometric method in the theory of analytic stacks, Proc. Nat. Acad. Sci.

results.name: Moduli of Curves
results.structural_conclusion: Families of algebraic curves can themselves be parametrized by algebraic varieties, revealing that moduli spaces have natural geometric structures. This establishes that classification problems in algebraic geometry have geometric solutions.
results.source_citation: Deligne, P. and Mumford, D. The irreducibility of the space of curves of given genus, Publ. Math. IHÉS

results.name: Intersection Theory
results.structural_conclusion: The intersection of algebraic subvarieties can be computed through purely algebraic methods using Chow rings, revealing that geometric intersection is fundamentally an algebraic operation. This shows that geometric intuition can be made completely rigorous through commutative algebra.

results.name: Flat Morphisms and Base Change
results.structural_conclusion: Families of algebraic varieties behave continuously when the family is flat, establishing the correct notion of continuous variation in algebraic geometry. This reveals that algebraic families have well-defined geometric limits and deformation theory.
results.source_citation: Grothendieck, A. Éléments de géométrie algébrique III, Publ. Math. IHÉS

results.name: Minimal Model Program
results.structural_conclusion: Every algebraic variety can be transformed through a canonical sequence of operations to a minimal model or Mori fiber space, revealing that varieties have canonical forms determined by their intrinsic geometry. This establishes a fundamental classification structure for higher-dimensional varieties.
results.source_citation: Kollár, J. et al. Flips and abundance for algebraic threefolds, Astérisque

results.name: Mirror Symmetry Conjecture
results.structural_conclusion: Calabi-Yau manifolds come in mirror pairs where complex and Kähler geometry are exchanged, suggesting a fundamental duality in the structure of these spaces. This reveals deep connections between algebraic geometry and mathematical physics through string theory.
results.source_citation: Candelas, P. et al. A pair of Calabi-Yau manifolds as an exactly soluble superconformal theory, Nuclear Phys. B

results.name: Hodge Theory for Algebraic Varieties
results.structural_conclusion: The cohomology of smooth projective varieties carries a natural mixed Hodge structure that encodes both topological and algebraic information. This reveals that algebraic varieties have intrinsic harmonic structures that bridge complex analysis and algebraic geometry.
results.source_citation: Deligne, P. Théorie de Hodge II, Publ. Math. IHÉS

results.name: Derived Categories of Coherent Sheaves
results.structural_conclusion: Algebraic varieties can be reconstructed from their derived categories of coherent sheaves, revealing that geometric objects are completely determined by their categorical structure. This establishes that algebraic geometry can be reformulated in purely categorical terms.
results.source_citation: Bondal, A. and Orlov, D. Semiorthogonal decomposition for algebraic varieties, arXiv:alg-geom/9506012

results.name: Étale Cohomology
results.structural_conclusion: Algebraic varieties over arbitrary fields carry topological invariants through étale cohomology, extending classical topology to arithmetic geometry. This reveals that geometric intuition from complex varieties applies universally across all ground fields.
results.source_citation: Artin, M., Grothendieck, A., Verdier, J.L. Théorie des topos et cohomologie étale des schémas, Lecture Notes in Math.

structural_conclusion: Algebraic geometry reveals that geometric objects are fundamentally algebraic in nature, with local algebraic data determining global geometric properties through sheaf cohomology and scheme theory. The field establishes deep dualities between geometric intuition and algebraic computation, showing that topological, arithmetic, and categorical structures are intimately connected. Modern developments suggest that algebraic varieties may be most naturally understood through their derived categories and moduli spaces, pointing toward a fully categorical foundation for geometry.
